summary: DHH announces the release of Rails 2.0 after approximately a year of development. The post is a brief, celebratory notice acknowledging his silence on the blog was due to the long crunch session. He expresses relief that the release is finally done after many near-misses, and mentions plans to update the Rails screencast.
key_points: Rails 2.0 released after roughly a year of development with many delayed deadlines
DHH's blog silence was due to intense focus on shipping the release
Plans to update the outdated Rails screencast as a next step
money_quotes: After about a year in development and oh-so-many we're-almost-there's.
key_insight: Rails 2.0 shipped after a grueling year-long development cycle, marking a major milestone for the framework.
